args.query:
SELECT "Index", 
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_count,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_count,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" = "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_count,
       COUNT(*) AS total_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE Date >= '2018-01-01' AND Date <= '2018-12-31' AND "Index" IN ('GSPTSE','NYA','IXIC')
GROUP BY "Index";

exec_args.sql:
SELECT "Index", 
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_count,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_count,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" = "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_count,
       COUNT(*) AS total_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE Date >= '2018-01-01' AND Date <= '2018-12-31' AND "Index" IN ('GSPTSE','NYA','IXIC')
GROUP BY "Index";

result.preview: "GSPTSE (up days: 44, down days: 41) and IXIC (up days: 44, down days: 31) had more up days than down days in 2018. NYA had more down days (36 up, 42 down)."

args.answer: GSPTSE (up days: 44, down days: 41) and IXIC (up days: 44, down days: 31) had more up days than down days in 2018. NYA had more down days (36 up, 42 down).
